opening_steps.title: A Tower No One Survives
body_lines: The tower has broken every ordinary expedition sent into it.
You have inherited a ruined hall and a debt so large the creditors have stopped asking politely.
Monsterhall is not a business plan. It is the only chance left before they decide your life is worth less than the balance.

opening_steps.title: A Hidden Hatchery
body_lines: Behind a collapsed wall, old guild markings lead to a sealed hatchery room still warm with tower magic.
The machinery is strange, but not hostile. It was built to care for something alive and bind it without breaking it.
You know enough old taming rites to understand what this means: monsters can survive the tower where hired delvers cannot.

opening_steps.title: The First Egg
body_lines: A storage cradle unlocks and reveals a single translucent egg humming with residue from the shallow floors.
When you lift it, a cracked brass thorn bites your thumb. One drop of blood falls across the shell and sinks into the glow.
The hatchery answers the bond. It is not treasure in the usual sense; it is a future worker, scout, guide, and companion.

opening_steps.title: The First Slime Companion
body_lines: The egg opens into a timid slime companion who studies the room with nervous curiosity.
The blood-mark holds. She listens when you speak, trusts the taming signs, and does not lash out like the tower-born monsters in old execution reports.
If one monster can be guided, then the creditors have a reason to keep you alive long enough to pay.

opening_steps.title: Make The Hall Useful
body_lines: One companion is a beginning, not a guild. You need a room where patrons can bring contracts and staff can work safely.
It will cost materials and coin, but a real service room can turn strange talent into steady income.
Build the first guild room and make Monsterhall operational.

opening_steps.title: The First Contract
body_lines: A creditor's witness arrives with a nervous local delver and a simple job: recover residue from a passage too narrow and caustic for humans.
Your slime companion is scared, but capable. This is exactly the kind of work Monsterhall was made to handle.
One fulfilled contract is enough to prove you can tame monsters, earn coin, and pay the debt instead of being buried under it.

camp_discovery_log: You found a sealed hatchery hidden in the ruined guild hall.
discovery_incubation_log: The hatchery responded to tower residue, old taming rites, and a drop of your blood.
hatch_loyalty_log: The blood-marked slime companion trusts your taming signs enough to work with the guild.
build_first_room_not_enough_resources_er: Not enough resources to build the first guild service room.
build_first_room_completion_log: You scraped together a practical guild room and made it ready for the first contract.
first_client_missing_monster_error: The first slime companion has not hatched yet.
first_client_completion_log_template: {name} completed the first contract and proved Monsterhall can pay the debt. She gained scouting and hospitality experience.
hatch_missing_monster_error: The hatchery did not create a slime companion.
first_hatch_log: The blood-marked egg opened into a timid slime companion with a talent for residue work.

debt_intro_with_debt_template: The guild is open because the creditors believe your tame monsters can earn. First debt due in {days} days: {gold} gold.
debt_intro_without_debt: The guild is open. Build, train, and support deeper expeditions.
debt_next_due_event_template: Next debt milestone: {name} due in {days} days.
debt_cleared_event: The current debt chain is cleared for now.

debt_missed_update_template: Missed {name}. New balance {gold} gold, due in {days} days.
